themes.hook: Most people watch Brent Crude. Smart investors watch the Strait of Hormuz. Because right now, your kitchen budget and India’s fiscal deficit are being decided by 21 miles of water.
key_data_points: Agro-chemical raw materials have spiked 15-20% due to West Asia tensions.
City Gas Distribution (CGD) sales volumes are projected to take an 8-10% hit.
6 critical LPG tankers are currently caught in diplomatic negotiations for passage.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The conflict isn't just an 'oil spike'—it's a direct tax on Indian rural productivity. While headline inflation looks at fuel, the real damage is the 20% surge in input costs for farmers that will hit food prices with a 3-month lag.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, given Zerodha's view on the broader economy, how should investors hedge against this 'geopolitical input inflation' that doesn't show up in headline crude prices?

source_articles.title: Iran war could affect fertilizer sector, impacting Indian agriculture
Middle East disruptions to impact daily CGD sales volume by 8-10%
India negotiating with Iran to move six LPG tankers through Hormuz

themes.hook: India’s retail investing boom has a massive, regulated hole in the middle. While Demat accounts are exploding, the pool of Registered Investment Advisers (RIAs) is actually shrinking.
key_data_points: Insurance distribution is seeing 30% agent attrition as the model becomes prohibitively expensive.
SEBI has officially flagged a decline in registered advisers alongside a rise in 'finfluencer' influence.
Digital acquisition costs for financial products are rising, straining traditional distribution margins.
themes.non_obvious_angle: We are witnessing the death of the 'middle-class' financial advisor. The regulatory cost of being an RIA is so high, and the commissions on 'safe' products so low, that the only people left talking to retail investors are unregulated influencers or high-churn bank agents.
themes.your_take_prompt: What's your take on the 'advice gap'—can tech actually replace a human RIA, or are we heading toward a systemic mis-selling crisis?

source_articles.title: SEBI chief flags decline in registered investment advisers
High commissions & 30% agent attrition strain India's insurance distribution

themes.hook: India’s solar power costs just ₹2.50/unit—lower than China and the US. But here’s the catch: having the cheapest power in the world means nothing if you can't move it across the street.
key_data_points: India's solar generation cost has hit a global low of ₹2.50 per unit.
Extreme weather (El Niño) is threatening hydro output just as industrial demand peaks.
State Discoms remain the 'weakest link' with significant operational efficiency gaps in recent rankings.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The 'Make in India' bottleneck isn't the cost of energy generation; it's the bankruptcy of the grid. We are over-investing in 'sexy' solar panels while the boring transmission infrastructure and state Discoms are structurally incapable of handling the load.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, do you think the private entry into nuclear power or the shift to InvITs for transmission can actually fix the Discom debt trap?

source_articles.title: Bernstein Says India's Solar Power Cost Is ₹2.50/unit, Lower Than China & US
India Power Sector: Demand Momentum Builds; Transmission Build-Out and El Niño Converge
Benchmarking Discom Performance: MoP’s 14th Integrated Rating

themes.hook: Crude oil is well below its 2008 highs, but the cost of 'Fuel Oil'—the stuff that powers container ships—has just surged past its 2022 peak. Globalization is getting an unannounced tax hike.
key_data_points: Fuel oil prices have surged above 2008 and 2022 peaks, despite stable Brent prices.
Rising memory prices are directly threatening margins in the Indian smartphone market.
35% of urban Indian consumers admitted to buying fake products last year as prices for 'branded' goods rise.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The 'Trade War' isn't just about Trump's tariffs; it's about a 'hidden' energy spike in shipping. When the cost of moving goods rises faster than the cost of making them, the competitive advantage of Indian MSME exporters evaporates, regardless of how cheap domestic labor is.
themes.your_take_prompt: How should Indian manufacturing companies rethink their supply chains if 'low-cost shipping' is no longer a permanent feature of the global economy?

source_articles.title: The cost of fuel oil has surged above the 2022 and 2008 peaks
Rising Memory Prices Threaten India's Smartphone Market
Shipping wars stitch higher costs into India’s apparel exports

themes.hook: Your AI pricing tool might be committing a crime. The CCI just issued a warning that most Indian tech founders are ignoring: 'Algorithmic Collusion' is the new anti-trust frontier.
key_data_points: The CCI has issued a guidance note advising enterprises to self-audit AI tools for competition risks.
Enterprises are increasingly bypassing traditional coding for low-code/no-code platforms.
A 48-year-old definition of 'industry' is being re-examined by the Supreme Court, potentially affecting millions of workers.
themes.non_obvious_angle: When every competitor in a sector uses the same 2 or 3 'dynamic pricing' algorithms, the market stops competing and starts behaving like a cartel—without a single human ever shaking hands in a backroom. The regulator is finally waking up to the fact that 'the code did it' is not a legal defense.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, as someone in a tech-heavy firm, how do we balance 'efficiency-seeking' algorithms with the legal risk of unintended price-fixing?

source_articles.title: CCI issues guidance note advising self-audits on AI tools to avoid competition risks
Why low-code and no-code platforms are gaining ground in enterprise software
